steps.title: Identify the decay factor
steps.content: The beam starts with $800$ photons. Decreasing by $50\%$ means multiplying by $0.5$ for each $7$ millimeters of material.

steps.title: Count the intervals
steps.content: At a depth of $x$ millimeters, the number of $7$-millimeter intervals is $\frac{x}{7}$. That makes the exponent $\frac{x}{7}$, not $x$.

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content: Use initial amount $800$, decay factor $0.5$, and exponent $\frac{x}{7}$. $$I=800(0.5)^{\frac{x}{7}}$$ The answer is D.
